slice.name: Truthful invocation effects
slice.purpose: Make command invocation effects explicit, truthful, and testable before any consolidation, retirement, or wider SynAIpse lifecycle work.
feedbackSignals: The consolidation audit found invocation-dependent writes hidden behind commands catalogued as safe reads.
The accepted plan requires a separate Linear admission, test-first proof, and independent QA before the first runtime implementation unit.
The Phase 0 independent QA receipt limits its findings to planning and structure, so runtime evidence must be created afresh on this branch.
slice.rootOperationalFailure: Agents cannot make safe delivery decisions when a recommended diagnostic invocation can change artifacts, repository state, Git state, or external-provider state without declaring that effect.

doctor.expectedEvidence: temporary-repository probe shows no artifact, Git, or repository mutation

docs-gate.expectedEvidence: validation command produces exit code and diagnostics without side effects

contract.expectedEvidence: contract analysis produces report without modifying workspace or repository state

context-health.expectedEvidence: health check reads context state without mutation

workflow:generate.expectedEvidence: generated workflow files written to .github/workflows; Git status shows new untracked files

pattern-scope.expectedEvidence: pattern analysis produces scope report without modifying repository

drift-gate.expectedEvidence: drift detection reports status without modifying workspace

check-environment.expectedEvidence: environment check reads system state without mutation

ui:fast.expectedEvidence: UI fast mode displays data without persisting state changes

ui:verify.expectedEvidence: UI verification checks state without modification

ui:explore.expectedEvidence: UI exploration mode reads and displays data without side effects

review-context.expectedEvidence: context review produces analysis report without mutating state

linear-gate.expectedEvidence: Linear API query returns issue status without mutating external state

plannedChange.implementationRule: Add tests and characterization before changing command behavior. Keep compatibility surfaces callable and make diagnostics pure by default where practical; effectful artifact output must be explicit, and every invocation declares all applicable effect classes.

nonGoals: Do not delete, retire, rename, or broadly refactor command surfaces.
Do not implement the proposed adopt or diagnose commands.
Do not widen PR #465 or modify its GitBook/context canary scope.
Do not mutate downstream repositories or external-provider configuration.
Do not claim CI, CodeRabbit, hosted provider, merge, or downstream adoption truth from local tests or this intent artifact.

validationPlan.command: jq -e '(.schemaVersion == "intent-artifact/v1") and (.linearIssue == "JSC-442") and (.reviewStatus == "pending") and (.plannedChange.effectTaxonomy | type == "array" and length > 0) and (.plannedChange.characterizationTargets | type == "array" and length > 0) and (.validationPlan | type == "array" and length > 0) and all(.validationPlan[]; (.command | type == "string" and length > 0) and (.expected | type == "string" and length > 0)) and (.reviewRequirements.intentReviewBeforeImplementation | type == "array" and length > 0) and (.reviewRequirements.implementationReviewBeforeIntegration | type == "array" and length > 0) and (.acceptanceCriteria | type == "array" and length > 0)' .harness/intent/synaipse-slice-1-truthful-invocation-effects-intent.json >/dev/null
validationPlan.expected: The admission artifact is syntactically valid, remains pending independent intent review, and retains non-empty effect, target, validation, review, and acceptance obligations.
The admission-only artifact satisfies the required documentation gate without claiming runtime behavior.

validationPlan.command: jq -e '(.plannedChange.invocationEffects | keys | length) == 13 and (.plannedChange.invocationEffects | to_entries | all(.value | (type == "object") and has("identity") and has("effectClasses") and has("authority") and has("retryPolicy") and has("rollback") and has("expectedEvidence") and (.effectClasses | type == "array" and length > 0)))' .harness/intent/synaipse-slice-1-truthful-invocation-effects-intent.json >/dev/null
validationPlan.expected: Every characterizationTargets entry has a corresponding invocationEffects record with required fields (identity, effectClasses, authority, retryPolicy, rollback, expectedEvidence), and all declared effectClasses are valid taxonomy values.
After implementation begins, focused characterization and negative undeclared-write tests pass before broader validation.
After runtime or artifact behavior changes, the deep behavior gate passes.
A nominally pure diagnostic leaves repository and Git state unchanged; any undeclared mutation fails the slice.

reviewRequirements.claimBoundary: This artifact and the draft PR admit test-first implementation planning only. Neither proves a runtime effect is fixed nor authorizes integration.
implementationReviewBeforeIntegration: current-SHA CodeRabbit review after an implementation commit
independent QA review of the current implementation SHA
all current-SHA CodeRabbit findings are resolved or explicitly waived
security findings are normalized and residual risk is recorded before integration

acceptanceCriteria: Every affected recommended invocation declares every applicable effect class and names targets or provider class, authority, retry policy, rollback, and expected evidence.

Complete target coverage: every characterizationTargets entry has a corresponding invocationEffects record with all required fields (identity, effectClasses, authority, retryPolicy, rollback, expectedEvidence).
Accurate per-invocation declarations: declared effect classes match actual observed behavior in characterization tests.
Current write paths are characterized before behavior is changed.
Negative tests reject or surface undeclared writes.
Catalog tests and harness next --json tests project truthful effects.
A temporary-repository probe detects mutation from a nominally pure diagnostic.
Independent QA reviews the current implementation SHA before an integration request.
Rollback removes the additive projection or behavior change while retaining characterization and regression tests.
